message:
feat(planner): expose extracted WHERE probe on AccessPath

Teach the planner to surface the WHERE-clause probe it already chose
during access-path costing instead of forcing downstream consumers
(connection seam, VDBE codegen) to re-extract the same expression from
the AST.

New public AccessPathProbe enum with three variants that mirror the
planner's internal decision:
- RowidEquality { target }      — `WHERE rowid = <expr>`
- Equality { column, target }   — `WHERE <col> = <expr>` backed by index
- Range   { column, lower, upper } — `WHERE <col> > .. AND <col> < ..`

AccessPath gains a matching `pub probe: Option<AccessPathProbe>` field;
all existing construction sites in `best_access_path_internal` are
populated with `probe: None` to preserve current behaviour, so this
change is purely additive.  Actual probe population happens in a
follow-up; this commit is the shape-only wiring so consumer crates can
start threading the field through.

The enum is marked `#[allow(clippy::derive_partial_eq_without_eq)]` for
consistency with AccessPath / AccessPlan and derives `Debug, Clone,
PartialEq` so planner tests can assert on probe shapes.
